description_long: This is a double-panel metal guard rail, featuring two symmetrical sections that meet at the center with a slight upward arch. Each panel consists of vertical rectangular bars evenly spaced and welded to two horizontal rails, one at the top and one at the bottom. The vertical bars extend slightly above the top rail, creating a decorative and functional barrier. The structure is robust and utilitarian, likely constructed from powder-coated steel for durability and weather resistance. The ends of the panels are squared off, with no visible flaring or special end treatment. The overall design is minimalistic, suitable for use in urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or residential driveways.
description: A double-panel black metal guard rail with vertical bars and a slight upward arch at the center, designed for urban safety and separation.
description_view_0: Front view showing both symmetrical panels, vertical bars, and the central upward arch.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the guard rail and the alignment of the vertical bars.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, displaying the vertical bars and central meeting point.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the depth of the vertical bars.
